title: TinyMLP-AI-DCC: AoI-CBR Joint Optimization for ETSI-Compliant Vehicular Beacon Control
objective: Evaluate whether a TinyMLP-based AI-DCC (Intelligent Decentralized Congestion Control) can simultaneously minimize Age-of-Information (AoI) and Channel Busy Ratio (CBR) compared to rule-based ETSI DCC baselines and the variable-beacon state-of-the-art, while satisfying MCU deployment constraints (memory < 16 KB, inference < 1 ms). A preceding Sensitivity Analysis phase (Phase 2-alpha) fixes default hyper-parameters before the main comparative experiment (Phase 2 main).

phases.purpose: One-at-a-Time (OAT) sensitivity sweep to determine default values for four key hyper-parameters (oracle cost weights, action grid size, CBR_target for Simplified Adaptive, TinyMLP hidden width) before the main experiment. Results are stored to paper/data/sensitivity/ and summarised in sensitivity_summary.json, which is used to confirm default parameters.
phases.protocol: One-at-a-Time (OAT): vary one parameter while keeping all others at their provisional default.

scenario.name: urban_grid
scenario.note: SumoNetSim 1.1.5 standard: 3x3 RSU grid (NUM_BLOCKS=3), EDGE_LENGTH=2400m, grid_size=7200m
scenario.note_density: density=20 veh/km/lane default; n_vehicles determined by sim_engine from density * total_road_length
scenario.note_seeds: Seeds reduced from 5 to 3 (user directive: small-step execution to minimize errors)

sweeps.description: Oracle cost weight alpha for AoI term in J = alpha*AoI + beta*CBR (beta = 1 - alpha). Controls the trade-off direction of the Oracle label generation.
sweeps.affects: Oracle label generation -> trained model policy

sweeps.description: Size of the discrete action grid |T| x |P| used in the Oracle grid-search and as model output classes. Affects both Oracle computation cost and model capacity.

value_details.3x3: T_GenCam in {0.1, 0.5, 1.0} s x p_tx in {-10, 0, +20} dBm = 9 combos
value_details.4x4: T_GenCam in {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0} s x p_tx in {-10, 0, +10, +20} dBm = 16 combos

sweeps.affects: Oracle exhaustive search cost and TinyMLP output dimensionality

sweeps.description: CBR target threshold used by BL-B Simplified Adaptive controller. Determines the operating point of the non-AI adaptive baseline.
Width h of both hidden layers of TinyMLP (two layers of same width). Controls model capacity and MCU memory footprint.
sweeps.affects: Model capacity, inference latency, MCU FLASH footprint

phases.selection_criterion: For each parameter, select the value minimising the combined score: 0.5 * normalised_AoI + 0.5 * normalised_CBR across 5-seed average.
phases.output_file_columns: param_value, seed, AoI_mean, CBR_mean, PDR_mean, runtime_sec

phases.purpose: Main comparative experiment: evaluate TinyMLP-AI-DCC against 4 baselines and 2 ablations across Urban Grid and Highway scenarios at multiple vehicle densities. Default parameters confirmed by Phase 2-alpha are used throughout.

scenarios.name: urban_grid
scenarios.description: SumoNetSim 1.1.5 standard: 3x3 RSU grid (NUM_BLOCKS=3), EDGE_LENGTH=2400m, grid_size=7200m x 7200m, 2 lanes per direction, traffic lights at RSU nodes
scenarios.speed_profile: 0-60 km/h (default 40 km/h), Krauss model with traffic light stops
scenarios.purpose: High-density + low-speed scenario; tests CBR overload control. SumoNetSim 1.1.5 standard grid.

scenarios.name: highway
scenarios.description: 5 km straight highway, 2 lanes per direction (4 lanes total), 80 km/h default, variable density
scenarios.purpose: High-speed + AoI-sensitive scenario; tests beacon rate adaptation. RSU unused (V2V only).

phases.total_main_runs_breakdown: 3 densities * 2 scenarios * 3 seeds * 7 methods = 126 runs (seeds reduced from 10 to 3, user directive: small-step execution)

fair_comparison_protocol.seed_fixing: Same 10 seeds applied to all methods via SUMO --seed argument
fair_comparison_protocol.scenario_files: Identical .sumocfg, .net.xml, .rou.xml for all methods per run
fair_comparison_protocol.phy_params: Nakagami-m propagation, alpha=2.0 path loss, 10 MHz, BPSK 1/2 = 3 Mbps for all
fair_comparison_protocol.statistics: 95% CI via t-test or bootstrap; boxplot visualisation
fair_comparison_protocol.bhattacharyya_params: Original paper CBR/neighbor thresholds preserved; p_tx fixed at +20 dBm

proposed.name: TinyMLP-AI-DCC
proposed.description: 2-layer MLP (input 5D -> hidden h -> hidden h -> output 8 logits reshaped to 4x2) trained offline via Behavior Cloning from an Oracle DCC table. Inputs: [speed v (m/s), acceleration a (m/s^2), estimated neighbour count N_est, CBR, AoI (ms)]. Outputs: T_GenCam in {0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1.0} s and p_tx in {-10, 0, +10, +20} dBm (argmax). Embedded directly in ETSI CAM generation layer; inference triggered once per CAM event. MCU target: STM32F407 Cortex-M4 @168 MHz; INT8 quantised; estimated <4 KB RAM, <1 ms inference.

architecture.hidden_width: h (confirmed by SA4, provisional default h=32)

training.oracle: Grid-search exhaustive over |T|x|P| action combinations, myopic 200ms horizon
training.framework: PyTorch (CPU), ONNX export, TFLite INT8 quantisation

baselines.name: ETSI DCC Reactive
baselines.description: ETSI EN 302 571 §8.1 Reactive algorithm. Three-state machine (Relaxed / Active / Restricted) driven by CBR thresholds (0.60 / 0.40). T_GenCam adjusted in 3 levels; p_tx fixed at +20 dBm. Implemented in etsi_cam_layer.py. Reference: ETSI EN 302 571 V1.2.1 (2014).
baselines.implementation: etsi_cam_layer.py — state machine with CBR hysteresis thresholds

baselines.name: Simplified Adaptive
baselines.description:
CBR-tracking proportional controller (simplified adaptive baseline). Replaces ETSI DCC Adaptive (LIMERIC) to reduce reproduction risk. Faithfully represents the Adaptive DCC family (congestion -> increase period) with deterministic, seed-stable behaviour. 
Algorithm pseudo-code (from idea_spec.md §10.1):
  Inputs : CBR_target = 0.60, T_min = 0.1 s, T_max = 1.0 s,
           step_size delta_T = 0.05 s,
           smoothing factor lambda_s = 0.5
  State  : T_GenCam (current beacon period), CBR_smoothed
  Per measurement window (every 100 ms):
     1) CBR_smoothed <- (1 - lambda_s) * CBR_smoothed + lambda_s * CBR_meas
     2) error <- CBR_smoothed - CBR_target
     3) if error > 0:                          # channel too busy
          T_GenCam <- min(T_GenCam + delta_T, T_max)   # increase period
        elif error < 0:
          T_GenCam <- max(T_GenCam - delta_T, T_min)   # decrease period
     4) p_tx held constant at +20 dBm
  CBR_target sweep range for SA3: [0.40, 0.50, 0.60, 0.70, 0.80]
  Default CBR_target confirmed by SA3 sensitivity sweep.

Paper statement: 'We adopt a simplified adaptive controller as a representative non-AI Adaptive baseline; full LIMERIC reproduction is left as future work due to its tuning sensitivity.'
baselines.implementation: etsi_cam_layer.py — proportional CBR controller with exponential smoothing
baselines.change_from_original: Replaces ETSI DCC Adaptive (LIMERIC) per user directive 2026-05-08 12:39 / idea_spec.md §10.1

baselines.name: Bhattacharyya2024 Variable Beacon
baselines.description: Variable beacon rate based on CBR and estimated neighbour count. Re-implemented faithfully from IEEE TVT doi:10.1109/TVT.2023.3307672 §III-B. No ML; heuristic rule-based; p_tx fixed. Paper statement: 'faithfully re-implemented based on [Bhattacharyya2024]'.
baselines.reference: Bhattacharyya et al., IEEE TVT 2024, doi:10.1109/TVT.2023.3307672
baselines.implementation: etsi_cam_layer.py extension — CBR + neighbour threshold lookup

baselines.name: Fixed 10 Hz
baselines.description: Static beacon: T_GenCam = 100 ms (10 Hz) fixed, p_tx = +20 dBm fixed. No congestion control. Serves as worst-case CBR upper bound and best-case beacon frequency lower bound for AoI.

ablations.name: Rate-Only
ablations.description: TinyMLP-AI-DCC with power control removed. T_GenCam output used normally; p_tx fixed at +20 dBm (no power adaptation). Isolates the contribution of joint power + rate control versus rate-only control. Trained on same Oracle dataset as TinyMLP-AI-DCC with p_tx dimension ignored.

ablations.name: No-AoI
ablations.description: TinyMLP-AI-DCC with AoI removed from input. Input reduced from 5D to 4D: [v, a, N_est, CBR] (AoI input zeroed/removed). Isolates the contribution of AoI as a feedback signal. Trained on same Oracle dataset as TinyMLP-AI-DCC with AoI column removed.
ablations.purpose: Quantify the value of AoI feedback in the control loop

metrics.name: mean_AoI
metrics.description: Average Age of Information across all neighbour pairs
metrics.measurement: aoi_tracker.py: per-CAM (receive_time - generation_timestamp); averaged over all (i,j) pairs every 100 ms evaluation step

metrics.name: CBR
metrics.description: Channel Busy Ratio — fraction of time the 802.11p channel is detected busy

metrics.name: PDR
metrics.description: Packet Delivery Ratio — fraction of CAMs successfully received within 100 m
metrics.measurement: SumoNetSim receive event log; count success per (sender, receiver) pair within distance threshold

metrics.name: energy_efficiency
metrics.measurement: p_tx level (from AI-DCC output or fixed baseline) x transmission count x packet duration / vehicle odometer

metrics.name: ETSI_compliance
metrics.description: Fraction of CAM generation events where T_GenCam is within ETSI EN 302 637-2 bounds [100ms, 1000ms]
metrics.formula: ETSI_compliance = (events with T_GenCam in [0.1, 1.0] s) / (total CAM events) * 100
metrics.measurement: CAM generation event log; T_GenCam range check per event

modules_to_implement.name: etsi_cam_layer.py
modules_to_implement.purpose: ETSI EN 302 637-2 CAM generation logic: event triggers (DeltaHeading >= 4 deg, DeltaPos >= 4 m, DeltaSpeed >= 0.5 m/s), T_GenCam timer management, ETSI DCC state machine (BL-A Reactive), Simplified Adaptive controller (BL-B), Bhattacharyya2024 variable beacon (BL-C), Fixed 10 Hz (BL-D). Interfaces with libsumo for vehicle state and SumoNetSim for channel stats.

modules_to_implement.name: ai_dcc_hook.py
modules_to_implement.purpose: TinyMLP inference hook that overrides ETSI DCC output. Loads ONNX/TFLite model, reads 5D input state from libsumo + aoi_tracker, runs inference, maps argmax outputs to (T_GenCam, p_tx) discrete values, applies ETSI range clamping (T_GenCam in [0.1, 1.0] s), and passes result to etsi_cam_layer.py timer.

modules_to_implement.name: aoi_tracker.py
modules_to_implement.purpose: Per-vehicle AoI tracking module. Maintains dict {(sender_id, receiver_id): last_gen_timestamp}. On CAM receive event: AoI_ij = t_rx - t_gen; updates running mean. On missed reception: AoI continues accumulating until next successful reception. Outputs per-step M1 (mean AoI) time series to CSV.

modules_to_implement.name: oracle_generator.py
modules_to_implement.purpose: Offline Oracle DCC table generator for Behavior Cloning training data. For each sampled state (v, a, N_est, CBR, AoI): exhaustively evaluates all |T|x|P| action combinations (16 by default) in a 200 ms libsumo simulation window, records AoI and CBR for each, computes J = alpha*AoI + beta*CBR, selects argmin action as label. Outputs oracle_dataset.npz (10000+ samples). Uses confirmed defaults from sensitivity_summary.json for alpha and action grid.

modules_to_implement.name: sensitivity_runner.py
modules_to_implement.purpose: Automated Phase 2-alpha sensitivity sweep orchestrator. Reads sweep definitions (SA1-SA4) from experiment_spec.json, launches libsumo + SumoNetSim runs for each (param_value, seed) combination, collects M1/M2/M3, writes SA<ID>_<param>_<metric>.csv files to paper/data/sensitivity/, computes selection criterion (0.5*norm_AoI + 0.5*norm_CBR) per sweep point, writes sensitivity_summary.json with confirmed default values. Supports parallel execution via Python multiprocessing.

execution_order: 1. Phase 2-alpha sensitivity sweep (4 sweeps SA1-SA4, ~54 runs total, Urban Grid density=20 veh/km/lane x 3 seeds per point)
2. Review sensitivity_summary.json; confirm default parameters: oracle_alpha, action_grid, cbr_target, mlp_hidden_width
3. Oracle dataset generation using confirmed defaults (oracle_generator.py, ~10000+ state-action pairs, est. 2-8h CPU)
4. TinyMLP training (PyTorch CPU, Behavior Cloning, confirmed h, <100 epochs)
5. ONNX export + TFLite INT8 quantisation + CMSIS-NN size verification (<16 KB)
6. Phase 2 main experiment: S1 Urban Grid (3 densities x 3 seeds x 7 methods = 63 runs) + S2 Highway (3 densities x 3 seeds x 7 methods = 63 runs) = 126 runs total
7. Statistical analysis: 95% CI (t-test / bootstrap), boxplots, significance tables
8. Paper writing: Performance Evaluation section with SA1-SA4 sensitivity subsection + main comparison

simulator.mobility: libsumo (SUMO Python API) — vehicle positions, speeds, accelerations
simulator.network: SumoNetSim — 802.11p MAC/PHY, Nakagami-m propagation, 10 MHz bandwidth, BPSK 1/2 = 3 Mbps
simulator.note: No external simulators assumed. CAM generation layer and DCC algorithms implemented directly in Python on top of libsumo. SumoNetSim provides channel statistics (CBR, PDR).

references.etsi_cam: ETSI EN 302 637-2 V1.3.1 — Intelligent Transport Systems; Vehicular Communications; Basic Set of Applications; Part 2: Specification of Cooperative Awareness Basic Service
references.etsi_dcc: ETSI EN 302 571 V1.2.1 — Intelligent Transport Systems; Radiocommunications equipment operating in the 5 855 MHz to 5 925 MHz frequency band; Harmonized EN
references.bhattacharyya2024: Bhattacharyya et al., IEEE Trans. Veh. Technol., doi:10.1109/TVT.2023.3307672

_metadata.user_directive_incorporated: 2026-05-08 12:39 — BL-B simplified to Simplified Adaptive (§10.1) + Sensitivity Analysis Phase 2-alpha added (§10.2); 2026-05-08 13:25 — scenarios+sweeps patched to SumoNetSim 1.1.5 standard (NUM_BLOCKS=3, EDGE_LENGTH=2400m, V2V=200m, RSU=800m, seeds 5→3, SA3 cbr_target [0.50-0.70], SA4 hidden_width [4-16] per idea_spec §10.2)
